text.title: Cross-Channel CPA Rollup Failure Modes
text.summary: Six antipatterns that make the rollup mislead budget allocation. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Sum of attributed_conversions across channels exceeds actual unique conversions in CRM.
antipattern.root-cause: Customer ID unstable across sessions, hashed inconsistently, or absent on key touchpoints.
antipattern.fix: Audit identity stitching: stable ID across sessions, consistent hash, present on every touchpoint. Re-run rollup. Rule violated: dedup-id.

antipattern.symptom: gross_spend equals ad-platform billing only; agency invoices for the same period are not added.
antipattern.root-cause: CPA looks artificially low because half the cost is missing.
antipattern.fix: Add agency + production fees to gross_spend. Re-evaluate channel viability — some flip from "winning" to "losing". Rule violated: gross-spend.

antipattern.symptom: View-through count added to attributed_conversions to make Meta CPA look better.
antipattern.root-cause: View-through inflates the count without evidence.
antipattern.fix: Keep view-through as a separate column. Report CPA with click-only attribution. Fund an incrementality test if view-through is critical. Rule violated: single-attribution-model.

antipattern.symptom: Branded keywords drive ≥30% of Google paid conversions; pausing branded paid would shift them to organic with minimal loss.
antipattern.root-cause: Paid claims credit for users who searched the brand directly.
antipattern.fix: Split branded vs non-branded into separate rows. Apply incrementality lens. Consider pausing branded paid (geo-test if uncertain). Rule violated: organic-baseline.

antipattern.symptom: cohort_d30 column blank across ≥3 channels for cohorts older than 30 days.
antipattern.root-cause: Channel allocation rewards low-quality acquisition because retention is invisible.
antipattern.fix: Wire the cohort retention computation. Define minimum viable signal (login, action, purchase). Rule violated: cohort-lens.

antipattern.symptom: attribution_policy.md modified more than once per quarter; historical rollup not re-run with new model.
antipattern.root-cause: Quietly switching "last click within 7 days" to "linear across 30 days" invalidates historical comparison.
antipattern.fix: Treat attribution changes like an experiment: version the model, re-run the last 90 days under both, document the delta, brief the team. Rule violated: single-attribution-model.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Channel-vendor dashboards may include narrative ("Meta lifted incremental conversions by 30%") that is not evidence. The rollup uses raw spend/conversion data, not vendor narratives.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Weekly review by growth lead + finance partner. The rollup is the artifact; the conversation is the gate. Budget shifts ≥ 20% across channels require two-week confirmation before commitment.
